title: cestrum fasciculatum
intro: Cestrum fasciculatum, also known as the night-blooming jasmine or night-scented jasmine, is a flowering plant native to the tropical and subtropical regions of Central and South America. The plant's medicinal properties lie in its ability to produce a toxic alkaloid called cesium, which has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Cestrum fasciculatum include its potential to reduce anxiety and stress due to its sedative properties, as well as its ability to lower blood pressure and reduce inflammation. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as teas and tinctures, can be used to harness its medicinal properties, although it's essential to note that the plant can be toxic in high doses and should be used under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
